title: RIO Commit Chain — Ledger + Integrity Substrate
description: Extracted from controlPlane.ts (A7: FormalLedgerEntry), kernelExecutor.ts (WAL discipline), integritySubstrate.ts (substrate checks), and db.ts (appendLedger, verifyHashChain).

FormalLedgerEntry.description: controlPlane.ts — A7: FORMALIZED LEDGER ENTRY. The canonical ledger block format. Each entry links to the previous via hash chain.

receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the witness receipt that produced this entry.

previous_ledger_hash.description: Hash of the previous ledger entry. Genesis uses 'GENESIS'.

entry_type.description: Discriminator for the ledger entry type.

payload.description: Type-specific payload. Schema varies by entry_type.

WALEntry.description: kernelExecutor.ts — WAL discipline entries. Three phases: PREPARED (before execution), COMMITTED (after success), FAILED (after failure).

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the WAL entry.

intentId.description: The intent this WAL entry belongs to.

WALPreparedPayload.description: Payload written to ledger as WAL_PREPARED before execution starts.

WALCommittedPayload.description: Payload written to ledger as WAL_COMMITTED after successful execution.

WALFailedPayload.description: Payload written to ledger as WAL_FAILED after failed execution.

SubstrateResult.description: integritySubstrate.ts — Aggregate result of all four substrate checks.

SubstrateInput.description: integritySubstrate.ts — Input to validateAtSubstrate(). The FIRST gate before any governance surface.

token_id.description: Optional execution token ID (for replay check).

GenesisRecord.description: authorityLayer.ts — Genesis record. Ledger block 0. Anchors the entire system.

policy_hash.description: SHA-256 of the initial governance policy.

hash_chain_integrity.description: db.ts verifyHashChain(). For every entry[i] where i > 0: entry[i].previous_ledger_hash === entry[i-1].current_hash. Violation = SYSTEM CORRUPTION.

wal_ordering.description: kernelExecutor.ts. WAL_PREPARED MUST be written before connector call. WAL_COMMITTED or WAL_FAILED MUST be written after. If PREPARED fails, execution MUST NOT proceed. If connector succeeds but COMMITTED fails, caller MUST NOT return success.

nonce_persistence.description: kernelExecutor.ts. Nonces are persisted to ledger as NONCE_CONSUMED entries. On restart, rebuildNonceCache() reads all NONCE_CONSUMED entries from ledger and repopulates the in-memory cache. Nonces survive restart.

substrate_gate_ordering.description: integritySubstrate.ts. validateAtSubstrate() runs BEFORE any governance surface sees the message. If any check fails, the message is BLOCKED and LOGGED at the substrate level. Governance surfaces never see it.

substrate_block_ledger_write.description: integritySubstrate.ts. Every blocked message writes a SUBSTRATE_BLOCK entry to the ledger with: event, content_hash, nonce, source, action, channel, block_reason, checks_failed[], timestamp.

dedup_ttl.description: integritySubstrate.ts. DEDUP_TTL_MS = 300000 (5 minutes). Same content hash within 5 minutes is a duplicate.

nonce_ttl.description: integritySubstrate.ts. NONCE_TTL_MS = 600000 (10 minutes). Nonces expire after 10 minutes in the in-memory store.

canonical_json.description: controlPlane.ts canonicalJsonStringify(). Keys sorted recursively. Used for all deterministic hashing.

hash_algorithm.description: SHA-256 via crypto.createHash('sha256'). Used for all hashes: envelope, receipt, ledger, action, policy.

failure_conditions.hash_chain_break: If entry[i].previous_ledger_hash !== entry[i-1].current_hash → SYSTEM CORRUPTION. Startup verification halts.
failure_conditions.wal_prepared_failure: If WAL PREPARED write fails → execution MUST NOT proceed. Return error immediately.
failure_conditions.wal_committed_failure: If execution succeeds but WAL COMMITTED write fails → caller MUST NOT return success.
failure_conditions.substrate_block: If any of the 4 substrate checks fail → message is BLOCKED. Governance never sees it.
failure_conditions.nonce_replay: If nonce has been used → BLOCKED_NONCE. Message rejected at substrate level.
failure_conditions.content_dedup: If content hash seen within DEDUP_TTL_MS → BLOCKED_DEDUP. Message rejected at substrate level.
failure_conditions.token_replay: If token bound to different content hash → BLOCKED_REPLAY. Message rejected at substrate level.
failure_conditions.receipt_linkage_missing: If source or action fields missing → receipt_linkage check fails. Message blocked.

authority_boundary_rule.rule: Execution requires a locally issued authorization. Upstream receipts may be consumed but never grant permission.
authority_boundary_rule.applies_to: Every ledger entry is a receipt. The next entry requires its own hash-link authorization (previous_ledger_hash binding). The chain does not grant write permission — the append operation does.
